operating_state: Total production reached record 1,826 MBOED in 2023, representing 5% growth driven by Lower 48 achieving record volumes from new wells across Delaware Basin, Midland Basin, Eagle Ford, and Bakken. International projects reached key milestones with first production ahead of schedule at subsea projects in Norway and China, plus startup of Montney's second phase central processing facility. Worldwide realized price decreased 27% to $58.39 per BOE with Brent averaging $82.62 per barrel and Henry Hub natural gas averaging $2.74 per MMBTU, resulting in net income decline to $11.0 billion despite higher sales volumes.
cost_structure: Generated $20 billion in operating cash flows despite lower commodity prices through disciplined cost management and production growth. Production and operating expenses increased due to higher well work activities and production volumes, while DD&A expenses increased from higher rates and reserve revisions. The company incurred $75 million in carbon pricing compliance costs across Norwegian, EU ETS, Canadian, and Alberta regimes, with additional costs anticipated from new U.S. EPA methane regulations requiring undetermined capital expenditures.
material_events: Completed $2.7 billion acquisition of remaining 50% Surmont working interest funded through new long-term debt, extending weighted average maturity from 15 to 17 years. Reached final investment decision on Willow project in Alaska following Department of Interior approval and began winter construction. Expanded LNG portfolio through equity acquisitions and 20-year offtake agreement at Saguero LNG facility. Returned $11 billion to shareholders through share repurchases, ordinary dividends, and variable return of capital payments.
macro_exposures: Faces heightened regulatory pressure from EPA's December 2023 final methane emissions rule and Australia's natural gas price cap legislation extended through June 2025. Subject to expanding carbon pricing mechanisms globally with uncertain cost trajectories and evolving sustainability reporting requirements. Exposed to escalating geopolitical tensions in Middle East and ongoing climate litigation from cities and counties seeking compensatory damages.
forward_outlook: Announced 2024 planned return of $9 billion to shareholders through three-tier framework including 58 cents ordinary dividend and 20 cents VROC for first quarter. Set 2024 capital expenditure guidance at $11.0 to $11.5 billion with production guidance of 1.91 to 1.95 MMBOED. Accelerated operational GHG emissions intensity reduction target to 50-60% from 2016 levels by 2030 and committed to net-zero Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2050.
uncertainty_profile: Reserve replacement ratio of 123% subject to commodity price volatility affecting economic life estimates and field decline rates. Environmental remediation obligations include CERCLA sites with accrued costs expected over 30 years, while legal costs increase defending climate-related lawsuits. Implementation timelines and costs for new EPA methane regulations remain uncertain, with cybersecurity threats posing increasing risks to operational technology systems.
